The '''Shikitari''' were a rare, [[Sentience/Legends|dim-witted]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the planet [[Shikitari]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. They possessed a multi-stage life cycle, and during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], very few individuals were aware of the Shikitari, with fewer still considered experts on their unique physiology.

==Biology and appearances==
This [[Sentience/Legends|dim-witted]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoids]] possessed a kind of unique physiology and had multi-stage life cycle. Shikitari were born in egg clusters suspended in a poisonous protective gel that warded off predators. As they hatched, the developing [[Pupa/Legends|pupae]] fused to one another, growing into a single combined lifeform. Individual Shikitari developed specialized body parts, becoming [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]], feelers, and so on. Shikitari pupae grew in a body style that mimicked their guardian – in the case of those raised by [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]], they adopted a similar morphology. However, this stage of the life cycle was extremely short, lasting only two standard [[Standard day/Legends|days]], after which they became encased in a hardened chrysalis<ref name="DB" /> from which a dozen harmless creatures, known as flutterscouts, emerged.<ref name="CE" />

==History==
During the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> very few individuals knew of Shikitari insectoids, and fewer still were counted as experts on the them specific physiology. The Shikitari were rare, [[Sentience/Legends|subsentient]] aliens found on a lush [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] world of the [[Shikitari|same name]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]].<ref name="DB" />

Shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> a [[Devaronian conman]] decided to carry out a scam that involved a Shikitari insectoid with the unlikely name of [[Carl]].<ref name="DB" /> This Shikitari was a collection of pupae, amalgamated into a humanoid form that was perfectly suited for [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] combat.<ref name="CE" /> The [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] entered Carl into the [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattataki]] battle games and pocketed a sizable price for him, snickering to himself that within two days his buyer would be the proud owner of a hardened chrysalis from which twelve harmless flutterscouts would emerge.<ref name="DB" /> Nevertheless, Carl died in [[the Cauldron]] arena before he could transition to the next biological stage.<ref name="CW" />

{{RPGseed}}
At some point, the Shikitari were engaged in a war with the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]]. During the conflict, a group of heroes were mistaken for the enemy combatants or hostile [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] by a [[Carrier/Legends|Chiss carrier]].<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref>
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The unnamed insectoid character first appeared in the [[Chapter 6 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|sixth chapter]]<ref name="CW" /> of ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', an animated television micro-series<ref name="Chapter Archive">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/archive.html|text=View ''Clone Wars'' Chapter Archive}}</ref> produced by [[Cartoon Network]] Studios<ref name="Volume One">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]]</ref> and originally aired on [[November 14]], [[2003]].<ref name="TV">{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-vi-276612|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 1, Episode 6: Chapter VI}}</ref> Just over [[2006|two and a half]] years later,<ref name="Penguin-Paperback">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/185065/star-wars-the-new-essential-guide-to-droids-by-daniel-wallace/9780345477590|text=Star Wars: The New Essential Guide to Droids}}</ref> the reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' mentioned a «Shikitari insectoid»; however, it did not confirm which specific character was being referred to.<ref name="EGD" /> Finally, Shikitari Carl was later given a name and short backstory in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CE" /> a [[2008]] reference book written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], [[Pablo Hidalgo]], [[Bob Vitas]], and [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="Penguin">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/160596/the-complete-star-wars-encyclopedia-by-stephen-j-sansweet-pablo-hidalgo-bob-vitas-daniel-wallace/9780345477637|text=The Complete Star Wars® Encyclopedia}}</ref>
